title: Measures of Central Tendency (mean, median, mode)

quiz_questions.question_text: The mean of a data set is calculated by:

options.text: Finding the middle value
Finding the most frequent value
Subtracting the smallest from the largest
Summing all values and dividing by the number of values

quiz_questions.explanation: The mean (arithmetic average) equals the sum of all data values divided by the count of values.

quiz_questions.question_text: The median of the data set {3, 7, 9, 15, 21} is:
quiz_questions.explanation: With 5 values already in order, the median is the middle (3rd) value, which is 9.
The mode is the value that appears most often. 12 appears three times, more than any other value.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which measure of central tendency is most affected by extreme outliers?

options.text: Median
None of them
Mode
Mean

quiz_questions.explanation: The mean includes every value in its calculation, so a single extreme outlier can pull it substantially higher or lower.

quiz_questions.question_text: For the data set {2, 4, 6, 8}, the mean is:
When a data set has an even number of values, the median is found by:

options.text: Averaging the two middle values
Multiplying the two middle values
Taking the higher of the two middle values
Taking the lower of the two middle values

quiz_questions.explanation: For an even-sized data set, the median is the average of the two middle values after the data are sorted.

quiz_questions.question_text: A data set can have:

options.text: No mode, one mode, or more than one mode
At most one mode
Exactly two modes always
A mode only if the data are numerical

quiz_questions.explanation: A data set can be unimodal (one mode), bimodal (two modes), multimodal (more than two), or have no mode if all values occur equally.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a perfectly symmetric distribution, the mean and median are:

options.text: The mean is always larger
Never equal
Equal
The median is always larger

quiz_questions.explanation: In a perfectly symmetric distribution, the mean and median coincide at the center.

quiz_questions.question_text: If every value in a data set is increased by 5, the mean will:

options.text: Increase by 5
Stay the same
Decrease by 5
Be multiplied by 5

quiz_questions.explanation: Adding a constant to every value shifts the mean by that same constant.

quiz_questions.question_text: The median of {10, 20, 30, 40} is:
quiz_questions.explanation: With 4 values, the median is the average of the 2nd and 3rd values: (20 + 30) / 2 = 25.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mean of a data set?

options.text: The most frequently occurring value
The arithmetic average, found by dividing the sum of all values by the number of values
The middle value when data are sorted
The difference between the largest and smallest values

quiz_questions.explanation: The mean is the arithmetic average: sum of all data values divided by the total count.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the median?

options.text: The middle value of a data set when arranged in order
The value that occurs most often
Half the range
The average of all values

quiz_questions.explanation: The median is the center value in an ordered data set, splitting it into two equal halves.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the mode?

options.text: The value that appears most frequently in a data set
The average of the highest and lowest values
The sum of all values
The square root of the variance

quiz_questions.explanation: The mode is the data value with the highest frequency of occurrence.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'bimodal' mean?

options.text: A data set where all values are equal
A data set with exactly one mode
A data set with no mode
A data set with two modes (two values that share the highest frequency)

quiz_questions.explanation: Bimodal means a distribution has two peaks or two values tied for the highest frequency.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'measure of central tendency' mean?

options.text: A single value that represents the center or typical value of a data set
A graphical display of data
The total number of data points
A measure of how spread out data are

quiz_questions.explanation: A measure of central tendency summarizes a data set with a single representative value around which the data cluster.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a weighted mean?

options.text: The same as the median
A mean calculated after removing outliers
A mean where each value is multiplied by a weight reflecting its importance or frequency before averaging
A mean where each data value contributes equally

quiz_questions.explanation: A weighted mean assigns different weights to values, computed as the sum of (weight times value) divided by the sum of weights.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'resistant measure' mean in statistics?

options.text: A statistic that changes with every new data point
A statistic that is not heavily influenced by extreme values or outliers
A measure that cannot be calculated
A measure that always equals zero

quiz_questions.explanation: The median is a resistant measure because it depends on position rather than magnitude, making it robust against outliers.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a trimmed mean?

options.text: A mean calculated only from the largest values
A mean calculated after removing a fixed percentage of the smallest and largest values
A mean rounded to the nearest integer
The same as the mode

quiz_questions.explanation: A trimmed mean removes a set percentage of extreme values from both ends before computing the average, reducing the influence of outliers.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'skewed distribution' mean?

options.text: A distribution where data are perfectly symmetric
A distribution where one tail is longer than the other, pulling the mean away from the median
A distribution that has exactly two modes
A distribution with no variability

quiz_questions.explanation: In a skewed distribution, data trail off more in one direction. Right-skewed distributions have the mean pulled above the median; left-skewed have it pulled below.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the midrange?

options.text: Half the standard deviation
The most common value in the data
The average of the maximum and minimum values in a data set
The same as the median

quiz_questions.explanation: The midrange = (max + min) / 2. It is a simple but outlier-sensitive measure of center.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher records test scores for 7 students: 68, 72, 75, 78, 80, 85, 92. She wants to report a single number that best represents typical performance. Which value is the mean, and is it a reasonable summary here?

options.text: Mean = 78.6; yes, the data are fairly symmetric with no extreme outliers
Mean = 75; the middle score is always the mean
Mean = 80; you just pick the most common score
Mean = 92; the highest score is the best summary

quiz_questions.explanation: Mean = (68+72+75+78+80+85+92)/7 = 550/7 ≈ 78.6. With no extreme outliers and a roughly symmetric spread, the mean is a good summary.

quiz_questions.question_text: A real estate website lists home prices in a neighborhood: $180k, $195k, $210k, $215k, $220k, $225k, and $1.2 million. A buyer asks for the 'average' home price. Why would the median be a better measure than the mean?

options.text: The mode should always be used for home prices
The $1.2 million outlier pulls the mean up to about $349k, far above the typical price; the median ($215k) better represents a typical home
The mean and median are always the same, so it doesn't matter
The mean is always better because it uses all data

quiz_questions.explanation: The mean = (180+195+210+215+220+225+1200)/7 ≈ $349k, inflated by the outlier. The median ($215k) is resistant to the outlier and more representative.

quiz_questions.question_text: A shoe store tracks daily sales of shoe sizes: 7, 7, 8, 8, 8, 9, 9, 10, 11. The manager wants to know which size to stock the most. Which measure of central tendency should the manager use?

options.text: The mode, because it identifies the most frequently sold size (size 8)
The mean, because it uses all data
The median, because it is in the middle
The range, because it shows the spread of sizes

quiz_questions.explanation: The mode (size 8, appearing 3 times) tells the manager which size is in highest demand, making it the most useful measure for inventory decisions.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student's grades are: Homework (weight 20%) = 90, Midterm (weight 30%) = 78, Final (weight 50%) = 84. What is the student's weighted mean grade?
A company reports that its employees' mean salary is $95,000, but the median salary is $62,000. What does this suggest about the salary distribution?

options.text: The distribution is right-skewed, with a few very high salaries pulling the mean above the median
Salaries are perfectly symmetric with values evenly distributed around the center
Most employees earn above $95,000 and the distribution clusters at the high end
The distribution is left-skewed with a long tail extending toward lower salary values

quiz_questions.explanation: When the mean is much larger than the median, the distribution is right-skewed. A few executives earning very high salaries pull the mean upward.

quiz_questions.question_text: A marathon had 200 runners. The mean finish time was 4 hours 15 minutes, the median was 4 hours 10 minutes, and the mode was 4 hours 5 minutes. If you wanted to predict the MOST COMMON finish time for future runners, which measure would you report?

options.text: The median, because it splits runners in half
The range, because it shows the fastest and slowest
The mean, because it is the most common statistic
The mode, because it represents the finish time achieved by the largest group of runners

quiz_questions.explanation: The mode (4:05) identifies the single most common finish time, making it the best predictor of the most likely individual outcome.

quiz_questions.question_text: A data set of daily temperatures (°F) for a week is: 55, 58, 60, 62, 61, 59, 57. A weather forecaster adds an erroneous reading of 160°F. How does this error affect the mean versus the median?

options.text: Both the mean and median increase dramatically
Neither is affected because 160 is obviously wrong
The mean increases dramatically (from about 58.9 to about 71.5), but the median changes only slightly
The median increases more than the mean

quiz_questions.explanation: Original mean ≈ 58.9. With 160 added, mean = (55+57+58+59+60+61+62+160)/8 = 572/8 = 71.5. The median shifts from 59.5 to 59.5 (still between 59 and 60), demonstrating its resistance to outliers.

quiz_questions.question_text: A survey of 10 households reports annual incomes: $30k, $35k, $38k, $40k, $42k, $45k, $48k, $50k, $55k, $500k. A politician claims the mean income is about $88k, proving the area is wealthy. An opponent counters that the median is only $43.5k. Who gives a more representative picture?

options.text: Both are equally misleading because neither the mean nor the median is appropriate
The politician, because the mean uses all data values and is therefore always better
The opponent, because the median ($43.5k) is not inflated by the single $500k outlier and better represents typical household income
Neither, because income data should never be summarized using a single number

quiz_questions.explanation: Mean = $883k/10 = $88.3k, heavily skewed by the $500k value. Median = (42+45)/2 = $43.5k, which better represents the typical household.

quiz_questions.question_text: A factory produces bolts with target diameter 10.0 mm. A sample of 6 bolts measures: 9.8, 9.9, 10.0, 10.0, 10.1, 10.2. The quality engineer computes the mean, median, and mode. Which of the following is correct?

options.text: Mean = 10.0, Median = 10.0, Mode = 10.0
Mean = 9.9, Median = 10.0, Mode = 10.2
Mean = 10.1, Median = 10.0, Mode = 9.9
Mean = 10.0, Median = 9.95, Mode = 10.1

quiz_questions.question_text: An ice cream shop tracks daily sales of five flavors over a month. Vanilla sold 450 units, Chocolate 430, Strawberry 200, Mint 180, and Mango 140. The owner wants to determine the most popular flavor and the average sales per flavor. What are the mode and the mean?

options.text: Mode = Vanilla (450 units); Mean = 280 units per flavor
Mode = Strawberry; Mean = 250 units
Mode = Mango; Mean = 280 units
Mode = Chocolate; Mean = 300 units

quiz_questions.explanation: The mode is Vanilla with the highest sales (450). Mean = (450+430+200+180+140)/5 = 1400/5 = 280 units per flavor.
